introduction: This is a parody of the '50s science fiction films which themselves were warnings of the unseen Communist spies in the midst of normal, healthy Americans. The film was made by over-dubbing the original film of the 1953 sci-fi turkey "Killers from Space" and adding some new scenes to it, to make something completely new which satirises the US “Don't ask, don't tell” policy on Gays in the Military.There are two main plots, one of which involves the US Military wishing to kill all gay men, originally by luring them to a free Barbara Streisand concert and dropping an atom bomb on them. There are further machinations in this vein after this plot fails due to usual American inefficiency of bomb-aiming and wipes out the town of Inbred, Texas instead.In the meantime, aliens from the planet Uranus, whose mission it is to make the universe gay and thus encourage peace and the arts, as well as defeating homophobia, turn the hero, played by Robert Graves into a raving queen. They want to use him as their agent for their gay-rays which can turn the whole planet gay. During continued attempts by the Military to deal with the “gay problem”, the Graves character, hypnotised by the aliens, works on their behalf to bring about a gay paradise on Earth.Winner of the BEST FEATURE AWARD at the Boston Underground Film Festival, and THE AUDIENCE AWARD at the Swiss Fantasy Film Festival, the film is so absurd as to be ravingly funny.
